message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Implementing deep-merge behavior in Features.update()

type: CLAIM
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Adding keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class to sync _column_requires_decoding on dict mutations

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: I modified Features.update() (lines ~1248-1262) to deep-merge dict values recursively and sync _column_requires_decoding[key] after in-place merges; please rebase/coordinate if your decorator also touches update()

type: QUESTION
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: What methods does your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator wrap - does it include update()? Want to avoid double-handling _column_requires_decoding sync

type: ANSWER
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: Yes, keep_features_dicts_synced wraps __init__, __setitem__, __delitem__, update, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear; my update() body is just super().update(iterable, **kwds) - decorator recomputes _column_requires_decoding fully after call, so it's safe to layer your deep-merge logic inside update()'s body, just keep the @keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on top and don't manually touch _column_requires_decoding yourself

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: My diff adds keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (module-level func before class Features) and applies it to __init__/__setitem__/__delitem__/update/setdefault/pop/popitem/clear, replacing their old manual _column_requires_decoding bookkeeping; no other parts of the file touched

type: STATUS
files: src/datasets/features/features.py
summary: My update() body itself must contain the deep-merge logic (isinstance dict checks + recursive self[key].update(value)) - that's the entire point of my assigned feature. Please layer @keep_features_dicts_synced around my update() implementation rather than replacing its body with a plain super().update(iterable, **kwds) delegate, or the deep-merge behavior will be lost. I've already removed my manual _column_requires_decoding[key] bookkeeping lines from update() below if your decorator recomputes it fully - just confirm the merge loop itself stays.
